settings.title: A Candlelit Winter Wedding in a City Hall
settings.summary: We planned a December evening wedding in a grand hall with short daylight to work around.
settings.description:
Grace and Michael chose a Victorian city hall in December and wanted a warm, candlelit feel against the short, dark afternoon. The hall was grand and high-ceilinged, which can read as cold and cavernous if the lighting and timing are not handled with care.

The daylight problem

The real constraint was the light. In December the usable daylight runs out early, which would normally cut across the part of the day when couple photographs happen. Rather than fight it, we built the whole timeline around it.

We set the ceremony at 2pm so that couple photographs landed in the last of the daylight, before it went. That single decision protected the photography without forcing anything to feel rushed, and it let the rest of the evening lean fully into the dark.

Building the warmth

Once the sun was down we filled the hall with the atmosphere the couple wanted:

  • Candlelight throughout to bring the scale of the room down
  • Warm uplighting to take the chill off the stone and plaster
  • A mulled drinks reception as it got dark outside

The result

The early sunset became part of the mood rather than a problem to solve. By the evening the hall felt snug instead of cavernous, and the December date turned out to be exactly the right choice for the warm, candlelit day Grace and Michael had in mind.
